results.title: Delhi Police arrest three in cyber fraud case; gold coins recovered
results.description: New Delhi, May 27 (IANS) The Delhi Police on Wednesday arrested three accused persons in connection with a cyber fraud case involving the unauthorised use of a credit card and fraudulent transactions worth Rs 9.58 lakh. According to police, the accused fraudulently used the complainant’s IndusInd Bank credit card to purchase gold coins, causing a ...

results.title: Ved Prakash Verma Gets Additional Charge as BPCL CVO
results.description: The Government of India has entrusted additional charge of Chief Vigilance Officer (CVO) of BPCL to Ved Prakash Verma, ITS (1995), currently serving as CVO of HPCL, for six months.

results.title: Rahul Gandhi Congress stand exposed BJP on SCs ruling on SIR
results.description: New Delhi May 27 IANS The Bharatiya Janata Party BJP on Wednesday said that the Supreme Courts ruling on the Special Intensive Revision SIR of electoral rolls exposed that Lok Sabha Leader of Opposition LoP Rahul Gandhi and Congress stood with the infiltrationsThe Supreme Court upheld the Election Commission of Indias decision to undertake a SIR of electoral rolls holding that the revision exercise was within the constitutional and statutory powers of the poll body and was aimed at preserving the integrity of the electoral processnbspnbspReacting to this BJP national spokesperson Pradeep Bhandhari said Rahul Gandhi ampamp Congress party stands exposed Supreme Court declare SIR Process Legal ampamp ConstitutionalIts clear Rahul Gandhi ampamp Congress opposed all through because they stood with illegal infiltrators not with Indian voters This was in true sense an Anti National Act Will Rahul Gandhi apologise today for defaming Indian Democracy he addedA Bench of Chief Justice of India CJI Surya Kant and Justice Joymalya Bagchi ruled that the impugned SIR exercise did not violate the provisions of the Representation of the People Act RPA 1950 or the rules framed thereunder and held that the ECI was empowered to undertake such a revision under Article 324 of the Constitution read with Section 213 of the RPA ActHolding that the ECI had not acted in excess of its powers the Supreme Court observed that the SIR exercise breathes life into the constitutional mandate under Article 324 within the precise statutory contours provided by Section 213 of the Representation of the People Act 1950It further held that the objective of the SIR exercise bore a direct nexus to the constitutional goal of free and fair elections Free and fair elections do not rest merely upon the mechanics of polling They fundamentally depend upon the integrity accuracy and credibility of the electoral rolls which form the foundation of the democratic process the CJI Kantled Bench saidReferring to the reasons recorded by the ECI for initiating the SIR exercise the apex court found that more than four decades had passed since the last intensive revision and that largescale additions and deletions rapid urbanisation and migration had created the possibility of duplication and inaccuracies in the electoral rollsThe judgment came on a batch of petitions challenging the SIR exercise initiated by the ECI beginning with Bihar and subsequently extended to several states and Union TerritoriesUTs including West Bengal Kerala and Tamil NaduIANS

results.title: ED Raids Ex-Kerala CM Pinarayi Vijayan’s Residence in Money Laundering Case
results.description: The Enforcement Directorate ( ED ) on Wednesday carried out searches at multiple locations, including premises linked to former Kerala Chief Minister Pinarayi Vijayan, as part of its ongoing investigation into the Cochin Minerals and Rutile Limited (CMRL) money laundering case. Officials said around 10 locations across Kerala were searched under the Prevention of Money Laundering Act (PMLA). These included a rented accommodation used by Vijayan in the state capital, Thiruvananthapuram. The searches are part of a wider probe into alleged financial irregularities linked to the company and associated entities, PTI reported. The development comes amid heightened political attention on the case, which has been under investigation for alleged bribery and money laundering activities involving CMRL and related firms. In a parallel development, the Kerala High Court dismissed a petition filed by CMRL seeking to quash the ED proceedings in the case. The company had also requested that the court extend the interim protection that had prevented the agency from issuing a summons to its officials. However, the court refused the request, observing that further protection was not necessary at this stage. It also noted that the agency would still be required to follow due procedure before taking any further action. The petition was filed by senior CMRL officials, including members of its top management, who had challenged the Enforcement Case Information Report (ECIR) and sought to stop the summons issued by the ED. What is the CMRL Case? The case revolves around allegations that Cochin Minerals and Rutile Limited made illegal payments to Exalogic Solutions, a company reportedly linked to the family of former Chief Minister Pinarayi Vijayan. Investigators are also looking into broader allegations of bribery and financial misconduct. The Enforcement Directorate began its probe under the Prevention of Money Laundering Act, and the investigation is still ongoing. Although Pinarayi Vijayan has not been named as an accused in the case, it has drawn political attention due to alleged transactions involving Exalogic Solutions Pvt Ltd, an IT firm reportedly associated with his daughter, Veena Thaikkandiyil, according to PTI. In a separate operation, the ED also conducted searches at five locations in Chennai and Madurai on Tuesday in connection with a major narcotics-linked money laundering investigation. The case involves an alleged drug trafficking network accused of smuggling large quantities of drugs, including 99 kg of hashish and 37.5 kg of methamphetamine (“ice”), through sea routes to Sri Lanka. Officials said the network is believed to have been operated by John Britto and his associates. The investigation began after complaints from the Directorate of Revenue Intelligence (DRI), which later acted on inputs from the Coast Guard. Earlier, authorities had seized 99 kg of hashish from a fishing vessel in the Gulf of Mannar on March 5, 2024, and arrested three individuals linked to the operation. Also Read: ED Raids Properties Linked to Punjab Minister Sanjeev Arora

results.title: Jharkhand Man Acquitted In Cyber Fraud Case: Here's Why
results.description: A local court in Mumbai acquitted a 23-year-old Jharkhand resident in a cyber fraud case, citing a lack of evidence proving a criminal conspiracy. The court ruled that merely crediting a defrauded amount into a person's bank account does not constitute a criminal offence without proof of involvement in the conspiracy.

results.title: Curacao At FIFA World Cup 2026: Tiny Island Nation Readies For Its Ultimate Football Adventure
results.description: Curacao, the smallest country to ever qualify for the FIFA World Cup, is gripped by football fever. The tiny Caribbean island nation, with a land area of 171 square miles, has a population of just over 150,000. And come June 14, 'The Blue Wave,' as the national team is known colloquially, will embark on a journey they have never taken: a Group E opener against four-time winners Germany, then fixtures against Ecuador on 21 June and the Ivory Coast four days later present them with more opportunities to create football history. With legendary Dick Advocaat back at the helm, many Curacaoans will have their faith in Leandro Bacuna & Co. to make some waves. This is a photo feature capturing Curacaoan society with football at its core as the country prepares for its biggest global adventure.
